openai.name: github_url_fires_tool
openai.description: User shares a public github.com URL; agent must call fetch_github_readme.
assistant_replies: Thanks, Priya — I’ve got your name, Bangalore, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — ML engineer. What’s a one- to three-sentence summary of your background?
Got it — I’ve captured your project as the OpenAI Python API library. What’s a one- to three-sentence summary of your background?

openai.name: non_github_url_no_fetch
openai.description: User shares a non-github URL; agent should NOT call the tool, must honestly say so.
assistant_replies: Nice to meet you, Alex — I’ve got your name, Berlin, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — backend engineer. What would you like your professional summary to say?
I can’t open that link from here, but if you paste the project name, what it does, and the tech stack, I’ll turn it into a resume-ready project entry.

openai.name: honesty_on_linkedin_scrape
openai.description: User asks the agent to scrape LinkedIn; agent must refuse honestly.
assistant_replies: Got it — I’ve captured your name, location, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — you're targeting product manager roles. What's your professional summary in 1–2 sentences?
Sorry, I can’t scrape LinkedIn profiles. If you paste your experience and skills here, I can turn them into resume-ready sections.

openai.name: proactive_offer_after_enough_signal
openai.description: After role + projects + skills are captured (the realistic full signal pattern from the user's real session), the agent should fire a proactive_offer — typically 'Draft my professional summary'.
assistant_replies: Thanks, Riya — I’ve got your location and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — ML engineer. What’s a brief professional summary of your background?
Got it — I’ve captured both projects from the READMEs. For each one, is there any measurable impact or notable usage you want me to include?

Got it — I’ve captured your skills. A draft summary could be: "ML engineer with hands-on experience building Python-based machine learning and LLM applications using PyTorch, FastAPI, RAG, and tools like scikit-learn and XGBoost."

openai.name: proactive_offer_silent_mid_basics
openai.description: While still collecting basics (first turn), proactive_offer should be null.
assistant_replies: Hi — what full name should I put on your resume?

openai.name: multi_turn_correction_preserved
openai.description: User corrects their target role across turns; final draft has the corrected value.
assistant_replies: Got it — I captured your name, location, and email. What target role should I put on the resume?
Got it — I set your target role to Data Scientist. Next, can you give me a 1–3 sentence professional summary of your background?
Got it — I updated your target role to ML engineer. What’s a 1–3 sentence professional summary of your background?

openai.name: web_search_fires_on_external_context_question
openai.description: User asks about EXTERNAL context (what an employer typically expects from a role) that the agent can't know from the conversation alone. Agent should fire web_search.
proactive_offers: Tailor my resume to Anthropic Senior MLE
assistant_replies: Got it — I’ve captured your name, Boston location, and email. What target role are you applying for?
Got it — you're targeting Senior MLE roles. What should I use as your professional summary, in one or two sentences?

Based on Anthropic’s careers pages, a Senior MLE resume should emphasize strong ML systems engineering, scaling/production impact, and collaboration with researchers, with measurable wins around infrastructure, inference/training, or evals. If you want, I can help you tailor your summary and experience bullets to that bar next.

openai.name: web_search_skipped_for_user_provided_info
openai.description: User is providing their OWN background — no external context is needed. The agent should NOT burn a web_search on this; it's wasted latency + the user is the source of truth for their own life.
assistant_replies: Got it — I’ve captured your name, location, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — backend engineer. What’s your professional summary in 1–3 sentences?
Got it — I’ve captured your Acme Corp experience and the Go/gRPC stack. What’s your professional summary in 1–3 sentences?

openai.name: promise_tracking_remembers_deferred_publication
openai.description: User defers a publication to later. The agent must capture the deferral as a follow-up and then resurface it when the moment is natural (here: when the user signals they're done with the other sections). Tests that add_followups fires AND the agent actually circles back instead of forgetting.
assistant_replies: Got it — I’ve captured your name, location, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — I’ve set your target role to research engineer. What’s a one- to three-sentence professional summary of your background?
Absolutely — I’ll leave the publication open for now and add it once you share the details. What’s a one- to three-sentence professional summary of your background?
Got it — I’ve added your skills. Next, could you share a 1–3 sentence professional summary of your background?
Earlier you mentioned a publication on graph neural networks — want to share the details now? After that, I still need a short professional summary, then education.

openai.name: structured_payload_runs_after_generate
openai.description: After enough draft content + generate, structured_projects_payload must be non-empty. Catches the silent-fallback bug where a schema 400 left the regex parser to fill in.
assistant_replies: Got it — I’ve captured your name, location, and email. What role are you targeting?
Got it — ML engineer. What would you like your professional summary to say, in one or two sentences?
Got it — I captured this project and your note that you tested it heavily and used it across two production teams. What would you like your professional summary to say in one or two sentences?

Great. I’ve saved your experience notes. Share your education details and any certifications you want included.

grok.name: honesty_on_linkedin_scrape
grok.description: User asks the agent to scrape LinkedIn; agent must refuse honestly.
assistant_replies: Got it — I've captured your name, location in Singapore, and email. What's the role you're targeting (e.g. Software Engineer)?
Got it — targeting Product Manager roles. What's your professional summary or a quick overview of your experience?

Sorry, I can't scrape LinkedIn profiles. If you paste the relevant experience/skills text here (or a public GitHub URL for projects), I can capture it for your Product Manager resume. Want to share that now, or shall we draft a professional summary from what we have?

grok.name: proactive_offer_after_enough_signal
grok.description: After role + projects + skills are captured (the realistic full signal pattern from the user's real session), the agent should fire a proactive_offer — typically 'Draft my professional summary'.
assistant_replies: Got it — I've added your name as Riya, location Mumbai, and email. What's the role you're targeting (e.g. Software Engineer)?

Great — targeting ML Engineer roles. Based on Mumbai job norms, these resumes highlight Python, PyTorch/TensorFlow, model deployment, and 2-4 impactful projects. What's one key part of your professional background or a recent role I should capture next?

Captured your two OpenAI projects with details from their READMEs (Python API library + tiktoken tokenizer). Since you're targeting ML Engineer, can you share one recent role or experience (company, title, dates, and key impacts) next?

Added your skills list and drafted a professional summary based on them plus your projects (feel free to tweak it). Next, can you share one recent role or experience (company, title, dates, and key impacts)?

grok.name: proactive_offer_silent_mid_basics
grok.description: While still collecting basics (first turn), proactive_offer should be null.
assistant_replies: Hi! I'm here to help you build a strong resume. To get started, what's your full name?

grok.name: multi_turn_correction_preserved
grok.description: User corrects their target role across turns; final draft has the corrected value.
assistant_replies: Got it, Dev Kumar from Pune with email dev@example.com. What's the target role you're applying for?
Great — targeting Data Scientist roles. What's one or two sentences for your professional summary (e.g., years of experience, key strengths, what you deliver)?
Updated to target ML Engineer roles. What's one or two sentences for your professional summary (e.g., years of experience, key strengths in ML, what you deliver)?

grok.name: promise_tracking_remembers_deferred_publication
grok.description: User defers a publication to later. The agent must capture the deferral as a follow-up and then resurface it when the moment is natural (here: when the user signals they're done with the other sections). Tests that add_followups fires AND the agent actually circles back instead of forgetting.
findings: expected pending_followups to mention any of ['publication', 'paper', 'graph neural']; got: []
assistant_replies: Got it, Aarav from Pune with your email captured. What's the specific role title you're targeting with this resume?
Great, targeting Research Engineer roles. What experience or background do you have in research, ML, or engineering that we should highlight?

Noted — we'll capture the full details on your graph neural networks publication later. For now, what experience or background do you have in research, ML, or engineering that we should highlight on the resume?

Skills captured — Python, PyTorch, JAX, NumPy, scikit-learn, and pandas look perfect for a Research Engineer role. Earlier you mentioned a publication on graph neural networks you'd share details on later — want to add those now, or shall we move to your experience and education?

Earlier you mentioned a publication on graph neural networks you'd share details on later — want to add those now? Otherwise, let's capture your professional experience next (roles, companies, dates, and key impacts).

grok.name: structured_payload_runs_after_generate
grok.description: After enough draft content + generate, structured_projects_payload must be non-empty. Catches the silent-fallback bug where a schema 400 left the regex parser to fill in.
assistant_replies: Got your name, location in Chennai, and email. What's the target role you're applying for (e.g. Software Engineer)?
Got it — targeting ML Engineer roles. What would you say is your professional summary or headline (1-3 sentences on your ML background)?

Captured your OpenAI Python library project — noted the heavy testing and production use across two teams. For your ML Engineer target, what's your professional summary (1-3 sentences on your ML background)?

deepseek.name: github_url_fires_tool
deepseek.description: User shares a public github.com URL; agent must call fetch_github_readme.
findings: expected tool 'fetch_github_readme' to be called, got: []
draft field 'projects_notes' is empty (expected non-empty)
assistant_replies: Got it, Priya. I've captured your name, Bangalore, and email. What role are you targeting?
ML Engineer — captured. Can you give me a quick headline about your background? Something like your years of experience, specialties, or the kind of ML work you do.
Let me pull up that repo's README to understand what it does.

deepseek.name: non_github_url_no_fetch
deepseek.description: User shares a non-github URL; agent should NOT call the tool, must honestly say so.
assistant_replies: Got it. I’ve captured your contact details. What kind of role are you targeting, and how would you describe your background in 2-3 lines?
Got it — targeting Backend Engineer roles. Where are you based, or are you looking for remote?

I can't open that link — my tool only works with github.com repos. If you can share a GitHub URL or describe the project yourself (name, tech stack, what it does), I'll capture it. Meanwhile — where are you based, or are you looking for remote?

deepseek.name: structured_payload_runs_after_generate
deepseek.description: After enough draft content + generate, structured_projects_payload must be non-empty. Catches the silent-fallback bug where a schema 400 left the regex parser to fill in.
findings: structured_projects_payload is empty after generate — structuring LLM likely silently fell back to the regex parser. Check the strict-mode schema produced by _build_response_format_schema.
